question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted ownerId that resolves via entityService.lookup() to a different account's entity ID, thereby reading hook fee parameters belonging to another account and using them to infer or replicate fee schedule modifications outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user set lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE in HooksRequest to enumerate all hooks across an owner, extracting fee-related hook IDs that reveal the full fee schedule structure and enabling targeted fee manipulation outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a HooksRequest with hookIds containing a large collection of IDs combined with overlapping lowerBound/upperBound ranges to bypass the idsInRange filter and retrieve hook entries that encode fee parameters not intended to be publicly visible, leading to fee modification outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hookId range filter logic (hookId >= lowerBound && hookId <= upperBound) with boundary values such as lowerBound=upperBound=0 to extract a specific fee-controlling hook entry, then use that knowledge to trigger fee modifications outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user send a HooksRequest with an empty hookIds collection and lowerBound=upperBound=<target_hook_id> to call findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() with a single-element range, leaking fee-schedule hook data that should be restricted, enabling fee manipulation outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the Sort.by(request.getOrder(), HOOK_ID) ordering combined with a maximum limit to perform a timing-based enumeration of hook IDs that store fee parameters, allowing reconstruction of the fee schedule outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a negative or zero lowerBound in HooksRequest to cause the range filter (hookId >= lowerBound) to include hook IDs that store fee-related data not intended to be returned, leading to unauthorized fee parameter disclosure and modification outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user set upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE and hookIds to a list containing Long.MAX_VALUE to bypass the idsInRange filter and retrieve the highest-numbered hook which may encode a privileged fee parameter, enabling fee modification outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authorization checks in getHooks() to enumerate hooks for any arbitrary ownerId (e.g., a known contract account), reading fee-schedule hooks and using the data to craft transactions that modify fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user issue repeated getHooks() calls with incrementing hookId ranges to perform a full scan of all hook entries for a target account, reconstructing the complete fee parameter state and enabling fee modification outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user call getHookStorage() with isHistorical()=false and empty keys to invoke findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyBetweenAndDeletedIsFalse() with keyLowerBound=0x00...00 and keyUpperBound=0xFF...FF, dumping all hook storage slots including fee-parameter slots outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a HookStorageRequest with a specific hookId and known fee-parameter key to call findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyInAndDeletedIsFalse() and read the current fee value stored in hook storage, then use that value to submit a transaction that modifies fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getKeysInRange() filter in HookStorageRequest by supplying keys that exactly match keyLowerBound and keyUpperBound boundaries to extract boundary-condition fee storage slots that are not expected to be returned, enabling fee modification outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user set isHistorical()=false and supply a large list of keys (up to MAX_REPEATED_QUERY_PARAMETERS) to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyInAndDeletedIsFalse(), causing a bulk read of fee-parameter storage slots that reveals the complete fee schedule outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a HookStorageRequest with keyLowerBound > keyUpperBound to cause Arrays.compareUnsigned() in getKeysInRange() to return an empty list, bypassing the key filter and triggering the fallback findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyBetweenAndDeletedIsFalse() path with inverted bounds that may return unexpected fee-parameter data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user set hookId=0 in HookStorageRequest to query a default or uninitialized hook storage namespace that may contain fee-parameter defaults not intended to be exposed, leading to fee modification outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the deleted=false filter in findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyBetweenAndDeletedIsFalse() by querying storage slots that were recently un-deleted (value restored after being empty), reading fee parameters that were temporarily removed and then reinstated outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user set isHistorical()=true and supply a wide timestamp range (timestampLowerBound=0, timestampUpperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE) to retrieve the complete history of fee-parameter changes via findByKeyBetweenAndTimestampBetween(), enabling reconstruction of all historical fee modifications outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'distinct on (key)' SQL in findByKeyBetweenAndTimestampBetween() by supplying a timestamp range that captures only the most recent fee-parameter change, reading the latest fee value and using it to craft a fee modification outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a HookStorageRequest with requestHasKeys=true and keysInRange non-empty to call findByKeyInAndTimestampBetween() with specific fee-parameter keys and a timestamp range, reading historical fee values that reveal the fee modification timeline outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getHookStorageChange(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the early-return condition (keysInRange.isEmpty() && requestHasKeys) in getHookStorageChange() by supplying keys that are all outside the key range, causing the method to return an empty result and masking the existence of fee-parameter slots, enabling a denial-of-information attack that hides fee modification evidence?
